steps.title: Equate the two expressions and form a quadratic
steps.content:
The two curves intersect where $y = x - c$ and $y = -4(x - 8)^2$ are equal. Setting them equal:

$$x - c = -4(x - 8)^2$$

Expand the right side: $-4(x - 8)^2 = -4(x^2 - 16x + 64) = -4x^2 + 64x - 256$

$$x - c = -4x^2 + 64x - 256$$

Rearrange to standard form:
$$4x^2 - 63x + (256 - c) = 0$$

The number of distinct real solutions to this quadratic equals the number of distinct intersection points.

steps.title: Apply the discriminant condition for two distinct solutions
steps.content:
For two distinct real solutions, the discriminant must be positive: $\Delta = b^2 - 4ac > 0$

$$\Delta = (-63)^2 - 4(4)(256 - c) > 0$$
$$3969 - 16(256 - c) > 0$$
$$3969 - 4096 + 16c > 0$$
$$16c - 127 > 0$$
$$c > \frac{127}{16}$$

Since $\frac{127}{16} \approx 7.94$, we need $c > 7.94$ for two distinct solutions.

steps.title: Identify the qualifying answer
steps.content:
Check each choice against the condition $c > 7.94$:
  • A) 3 — No, too small
  • B) 7 — No, too small
  • C) $\frac{127}{16} \approx 7.94$ — No, this equals the boundary (one solution, not two)
  • D) 13 — Yes, \$13 > 7.94$ ✓

That matches choice D — D is correct.
